articles.title: TORNADO CASH DEV ROMAN STORM FOUND GUILTY ON ONE COUNT
articles.summary: A New York jury found Tornado Cash developer Roman Storm guilty of operating an unlicensed money transmitting business, but they acquitted him of sanctions violations and were deadlocked on money laundering charges. The partial verdict spares Storm from a potential 20-year sentence but leaves him facing up to five years. The defense counsel argued Storm had no control over the decentralized protocol, which was used for both privacy and illicit purposes. The decision is seen as a partial win for crypto developers, highlighting the legal ambiguity around building open-source privacy tools. Sentencing is pending, and the DOJ may still retry the unresolved charge.

articles.title: SEC GREENLIGHTS LIQUID STAKING, UNLOCKS INSTITUTIONAL PARTICIPATION
articles.summary: The SEC clarified that receipt tokens from liquid staking aren't securities if the underlying activities are “ministerial or administrative,” effectively greenlighting protocol-driven LST platforms. This unlocks institutional strategies like earning 5–15% staking yields while maintaining liquidity, solving a key treasury obstacle. Liquid restaking on Ethereum has surged to $24B TVL, and the new guidance is expected to accelerate that growth.

articles.title: AXAL – A SMARTER WAY TO SAVE
articles.summary: Axal is a new stablecoin savings account leveraging DeFi positions to earn a 6-10% yield. Axal acts as a curator of protocols and automatically allocates funds to the appropriate pools to earn high yield at adjusted risk. Currently, the yield sources include Morpho, Aave, and Euler, though there are plans to further expand the strategies and yield sources.

articles.title: INTRODUCING THE SUPERFORM FOUNDATION
articles.summary: The Superform Foundation was established as a legally independent Cayman foundation to guide Superform's permissionless infrastructure and accelerate decentralization of its consumer finance protocols. It will steward core infrastructure, expand the ecosystem through integrations, grants, and incentive programs, and develop governance and token-utility frameworks for $UP holders. The Foundation will also drive a gradual shift toward community-led governance with full transparency over decisions, treasury operations, and roadmap priorities. Upcoming deliverables include a tokenomics paper, protocol documentation, core governance proposals, and activation of token functionality to coordinate protocol growth.

articles.title: BALAJI ON TOKENIZED IPOS
articles.summary: Lina Khan's decision to block a major acquisition forced startups like Figma to scale independently, creating outsized value for employees and investors. While some argue Adobe's purchase might have delivered greater returns, proponents counter that in a power-law world, you only need one home run to justify a policy. Balaji calls for easier IPO access, ideally via crypto-native equity markets, to reverse the decline in US listings and avoid the regulatory complexity that entrenches Big Tech. He warns that antitrust interventions and baroque deal structures ultimately strengthen incumbents and that crypto exchanges could supplant traditional capital markets.

articles.title: BEST HYPE YIELDS
articles.summary: beHYPE campaigns on HyperBeat (20% of the airdrop) and Spectra Finance (29% APR until August 30) reward early depositors with tradable points via Rumpel Labs. Leveraged wstHYPE farming on Sentiment can net an 82.5% APR looped yield, while Pendle offers a 3.11% ROI over 85 days for long-term exposure with exit optionality. Felix Protocol's stability pool provides 16% organic APR on feUSD and discounted HYPE from liquidations. Advanced users can deploy delta-neutral positions – combining fixed-rate farming with shorting – to lock in 15-26% net yields while farming additional ecosystem points.

articles.title: HYPERLIQUID: THE ECONOMICS OF TRUST
articles.summary: Hyperliquid addresses the industry's “extraction meta,” where speculative token launches and founder dumps erode market trust, by operating without private backers and sustaining continuous buybacks through its Assistance Fund. Its buyback rate is set to repurchase the entire circulating supply in under four years, backed by real revenue and an integrity-driven team, which attracts serious, long-term holders. By aligning incentives around transparent, risk-capital commitment rather than hype-driven speculation, it restores the foundational trust that underpins viable crypto markets. In doing so, Hyperliquid positions itself as a rare, credible store-of-value token amid a landscape of extractive projects.

articles.title: AAVE BASE USDC DEPOSITS HIT $200M AFTER INCENTIVE CAMPAIGN
articles.summary: USDC deposits on Aave's Base deployment increased 22.7% to a record $200M following the launch of the Base Incentive Campaign on July 23. Framed as an ongoing “arms race” for DeFi dominance on Base, the campaign pits Aave's yield-backed growth model against competitors allegedly burning governance tokens. While the rivalry unfolds, users benefit from the boosted incentives and attractive yields, evidence of how protocol wars can drive adoption.

articles.title: BASE CHAIN 30-MINUTE OUTAGE
articles.summary: After nearly two years of uninterrupted operation, Base chain experienced a 33-minute pause in block production due to a handoff to an unhealthy sequencer.

articles.title: MEI PHARMA BUYS $100 MILLION OF LITECOIN
articles.summary: MEI Pharma, a Nasdaq-listed company, bought 929,548 LTC worth $100 million as a treasury asset.

articles.title: ETHEREUM TREASURY FIRMS ARE NOW “VERY INVESTABLE”
articles.summary: Ethereum treasury companies are now more attractive than U.S. spot ETH ETFs because of staking yield, DeFi access, and normalized NAV multiples above 1.0.

articles.title: TOM LEE'S ETH BET COULD RESHAPE THE FINANCIAL ORDER
articles.summary: BitMine's Tom Lee is accumulating ETH 12x faster than Saylor's BTC pace, aiming to own 5% of the supply (~6M ETH) by 2026.
